WARNING: This data is ONLY for CSE and CS branch.
document: Non-Credit (NC) and AEC Courses - 3rd and 4th Semester

courses.note: Non-Credit subject. MSE2 = 25 marks only. ESE = 25 marks.

units.title: Basic Information about Indian Constitution
topics: Meaning of constitution law and constitutionalism
Historical Background of Constituent Assembly
Government of India Act of 1935 and Indian Independence Act of 1947
Enforcement of the Constitution, Salient Features
Preamble, Fundamental Rights, Fundamental Duties
Directive Principles of State Policy
Parliamentary System, Federal System, Centre-State Relations
Amendment procedure, Emergency Provisions
Local Self Government - Constitutional Scheme in India

units.title: Union Executive and State Executive
topics: Powers of Indian Parliament, Rajya Sabha, Lok Sabha
Powers and Functions of the President
Powers and Functions of the Prime Minister
Independence of Supreme Court, Judicial Review, PIL
Lok Pal, Lok Ayukta
State Executives: Governor, Chief Minister, State Legislature, High Court

units.title: Basic Information about Legal System
topics: Sources of Law and the Court Structure
Acts of Parliament, Common Law, Case law
Court System in India (District Court, High Courts, Supreme Court)
Arbitration as alternative dispute resolution
Contract law, Tort, Law at workplace

units.title: Election provisions, Emergency provisions, Amendment of the constitution
topics: Election Commission of India - composition, powers, electoral process
Types of emergency: grounds, procedure, duration, effects
Amendment of the constitution: meaning, procedure and limitations

courses.note: No MSE conducted for this subject.

units.title: Series, Coding and Decoding
topics: Importance and overview of Quantitative Aptitude
Number Series, Letter Series, Analogies
Coding and Decoding

units.title: Data Arrangement
topics: Ranking and Order, Direction Sense
Linear and Circular sitting arrangement

units.title: Blood Relation and Puzzles
topics: Basic concepts of blood relationships
Conversation based blood relationships
Family Tree based problems
Coded relationships and related puzzles

units.title: Critical and Non Verbal Reasoning
topics: Statement arguments, course of action
Classification and grouping of images, Figure series
Mirror image, Water image, Paper cutting, Paper folding
Embedded figures

units.title: Foundation of Communication and Self-Awareness
topics: British Council-English Score Test
Team Presentations on Change Management Models
Presentations on Personality Profiling for professional growth

units.title: Verbal Communication and Clarity
topics: Pronunciation Drill 1 & 2
Elevator Pitch Practice Session 1 & 2

units.title: Professionalism and Workplace Readiness
topics: Professional Grooming and Etiquette
Group Discussion (General Topics)
Panel Discussion on workplace scenarios using caselets

units.title: Introduction to Value Education
topics: Understanding the need, guidelines, content, and process for Value Education
Natural Acceptance and Experiential Validation
Continuous Happiness and Prosperity
Right understanding, Relationship, and Physical Facilities
Understanding Happiness and Prosperity - critical appraisal of current scenario

units.title: Understanding Harmony in the Human Being
topics: Human being as co-existence of sentient I and material Body
Needs of Self (I) and Body: Sukh and Suvidha
Body as instrument of I
Sanyam and Swasthya; correct appraisal of Physical needs

units.title: Understanding Harmony in the Family and Society
topics: Harmony in the Family as basic unit of human interaction
Trust (Vishwas) and Respect (Samman) as foundational values
Undivided Society (Akhand Samaj), Universal Order (Sarvabhaum Vyawastha)

units.title: Understanding Harmony in Nature and Existence
topics: Whole existence as Co-existence
Harmony in Nature: Inter connectedness, mutual fulfillment
Recyclability and self-regulation in nature
Existence as Co-existence (Sah-astitva)

units.title: Implications on Professional Ethics
topics: Natural acceptance of human values
Definiteness of Ethical Human Conduct
Basis for Humanistic Education
Humanistic Universal Order
Competence in Professional Ethics

courses.textbook: R R Gaur, R Asthana, G P Bagaria - A Foundation Course in Human Values and Professional Ethics (2nd Revised Edition), Excel Books, New Delhi. ISBN 978-93-87034-47-1

courses.note: No MSE conducted for this subject.

units.title: Analytical Reasoning & Logical Puzzles
topics: Concept and Relation of Cube and Cuboids
Cut the cube in different layers
Problems related with open and closed dice

units.title: Syllogism
topics: Understanding of Venn diagram
Problems related with Venn diagram
Statement and Conclusion
Syllogism and reverse syllogism

units.title: Clock and Calendar
topics: Concept and Relation of angle and time
Overtaking, overlapping, right-angle and straight Angle
Error in clock (faster and slower)
Mirror and Water Image of clock
Normal and Leap Year, Finding Odd days
Finding the day of the week of given date

units.title: Data Interpretation and Critical Reasoning
topics: Tables, Line Graph, Bar Graph, Pi Chart, Mixed Graph
Critical Reasoning: Assumptions, Cause and Effect
Assertion and Reason, Statement and Inference

courses.prerequisite: Successful completion of Soft Skills Essentials-1 (3rd Semester)

units.title: Advanced Communication and Cultural Sensitivity
topics: Vocabulary Enhancement through Gamification
Story Coining and Presentations
Understanding Cross-Cultural Communication (DEI) using Case Studies
Duo Lingo English Proficiency Tests

units.title: Professional Expression and Digital Literacy
topics: TMAY through Driver's test
Writing Effective Prompts on Various LLMs
Duo Lingo English Proficiency Tests

units.title: Interpersonal Effectiveness and Emotional Intelligence
topics: Negotiation & Persuasion Role Plays
Developing Emotional Intelligence via Scenario-Based Discussions
